faults.name: Floating or incorrectly biased non-inverting input
faults.why: A missing 0 V reference on the non-inverting input can force the output into rail saturation even when the feedback ratio is correct.
faults.verification_test: Measure the non-inverting input with respect to circuit ground.
faults.fix_recipe: Power off, tie the non-inverting input to circuit ground, power on, and retest Vout.

faults.name: Open or wrong feedback path
faults.why: Without negative feedback, the op-amp goes open-loop and saturates.
faults.verification_test: Power off and ohm-meter Rf between Vout and the inverting input node.
faults.fix_recipe: Reconnect Rf from Vout to the inverting node and verify resistor value.

faults.name: Power rail or ground problem
faults.why: A missing or unequal rail can clamp the output or move the apparent signal reference.
faults.verification_test: Measure both rails with respect to circuit ground.
faults.fix_recipe: Restore symmetric rails and a shared ground before changing signal components.
